Loading the data

# Data handling
library(dplyr)      # data wrangling (filter, mutate, summarize, etc.)
library(tidyr)      # data reshaping and cleaning

# Visualization
library(ggplot2)    # data visualization
library(GGally)     # scatterplot matrices, correlations (pairs plot)
library(corrplot)   # correlation matrix plots

# --- Packages (add any you don't already have) ---
library(scales)
library(factoextra)
library(tibble)

library(Hotelling)
library(profileR)

library(tidyverse)
library(CCA)
library(CCP)


#load the data
#change file path of yours
genom_data <- read.csv("/Users/won-cheolchang/Desktop/PSU/07. 2025 Fall/Project 1/humanAR_1mbData.csv")

EDA

# This block is used for data cleaning/summary

#use below code check the null data

#colSums(is.na(genom_data)) #null data check

# changing to factors 
genom_data$state <- as.factor(genom_data$state)
genom_data$chr <- as.factor(genom_data$chr)

# use below code to select continous variables
#cont_vars <- genom_data %>%
#  dplyr::select(ins.std, del.std, sub.std, microsat.std, GC, CpG, nCGm, LINE, SINE, NLp)

# use below code for numerical summary
# summary(cont_vars)
# Boxplots for the four standardized mutation rates

# Reshape to long format for ggplot


genom_data_long <- genom_data %>%
  dplyr::select(ins.std, del.std, sub.std, microsat.std) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(),
               names_to = "Mutation_Type",
               values_to = "Value")

ggplot(genom_data_long, aes(x = Mutation_Type, y = Value, fill = Mutation_Type)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(title = "Boxplots of Standardized Mutation Rates",
       x = "Mutation Type",
       y = "Standardized Value") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.position = "none"  # remove legend since x-axis already labels
  )

# Histograms for genomic features (each with its own axis)
genom_data_long2 <- genom_data %>%
  dplyr::select(GC, CpG, nCGm, LINE, SINE, NLp) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(),
               names_to = "Feature",
               values_to = "Value")

ggplot(genom_data_long2, aes(x = Value, fill = Feature)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, alpha = 0.7, color = "black") +
  facet_wrap(~Feature, scales = "free") +   # free scales for each feature
  labs(title = "Histograms of Genomic Features",
       x = "Value",
       y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

# Filter 4 mutation rate variables
mut_rates <- genom_data %>% dplyr::select(ins.std, del.std, sub.std, microsat.std)



# Scatterplot matrix to visualize pairwise co-variation
GGally::ggpairs(mut_rates)

# Select only relevant columns for plotting
mut_data <- genom_data %>%
  dplyr::select(state, ins.std, del.std, sub.std, microsat.std)

# Convert data from wide to long format for ggplot
mut_long <- mut_data %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(ins.std, del.std, sub.std, microsat.std),
               names_to = "MutationType",
               values_to = "Rate")

# Create boxplots for each mutation type per state
ggplot(mut_long, aes(x = state, y = Rate, fill = state)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~ MutationType, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Mutation Rates by Divergence State",
       x = "Divergence State",
       y = "Standardized Mutation Rate") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

# Correlation heatmap of all numerical variables

library(corrplot)

# Select numeric variables
num_vars <- genom_data %>%
  dplyr::select(where(is.numeric))

# Desired order: all features first, then mutation rates
var_order <- c("GC", "CpG", "nCGm", "LINE", "SINE", "NLp",
               "ins.std", "del.std", "sub.std", "microsat.std")

# Reorder correlation matrix
cor_matrix <- cor(num_vars, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
cor_matrix <- cor_matrix[var_order, var_order]

# Heatmap with fixed order
corrplot(cor_matrix, method = "color",
         type = "upper", order = "original",
         tl.col = "black", tl.srt = 45,
         addCoef.col = "black", number.cex = 0.6,
         col = colorRampPalette(c("blue", "white", "red"))(200))

# PCA 
# --- 1) Prepare data ---
mut_vars = c("ins.std", "del.std", "sub.std", "microsat.std")

X = genom_data %>%
  dplyr::select(all_of(mut_vars)) %>%
  drop_na()

# --- 2) PCA (variables are already standardized; center again just in case) ---
pca = prcomp(X, center = TRUE, scale. = FALSE)

summary(pca)
## Importance of components:
##                           PC1    PC2     PC3     PC4
## Standard deviation     1.8517 1.1911 0.72331 0.56561
## Proportion of Variance 0.6026 0.2493 0.09194 0.05622
## Cumulative Proportion  0.6026 0.8518 0.94378 1.00000
round(pca$rotation, 3)
##                PC1    PC2    PC3    PC4
## ins.std      0.556  0.024 -0.717  0.419
## del.std      0.564  0.033 -0.042 -0.824
## sub.std      0.607  0.033  0.696  0.382
## microsat.std 0.052 -0.999  0.005 -0.004
# --- 3) Scree plot ---
fviz_eig(pca, 
         geom = "line",
         addlabels = TRUE,
         ggtheme = theme_minimal(),
         ylim = c(0,80))+
ggtitle("Screeplot of PCA")

# --- 4) PC1–PC2 biplot (scores + variable loading arrows) ---
fviz_pca_biplot(pca,
                col.var = "blue",    
                col.ind = "yellow",
                repel = TRUE # Avoids text overlapping
)+
ggtitle("PCA Biplot of Mutation Rates")

# MANOVA
# --- 0) Prep ---
mut_vars = c("ins.std","del.std","sub.std","microsat.std")


# Keep only complete cases on variables we use
dat = genom_data %>%
  dplyr::select(state, all_of(mut_vars)) %>%
  drop_na()

# --- 1) MANOVA: Do mutation rates differ across the 6 states? ---
fit_manova = manova(cbind(ins.std, del.std, sub.std, microsat.std) ~ state, data = dat)

# Multivariate tests (Wilks, Pillai, etc.)
summary(fit_manova, test = "Wilks")
##             Df   Wilks approx F num Df den Df    Pr(>F)    
## state        5 0.37633   154.92     20 9042.1 < 2.2e-16 ***
## Residuals 2729                                             
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
summary(fit_manova, test = "Pillai")
##             Df  Pillai approx F num Df den Df    Pr(>F)    
## state        5 0.82462   141.74     20  10916 < 2.2e-16 ***
## Residuals 2729                                             
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
summary(fit_manova, test = "Hotelling-Lawley")
##             Df Hotelling-Lawley approx F num Df den Df    Pr(>F)    
## state        5            1.175   160.06     20  10898 < 2.2e-16 ***
## Residuals 2729                                                      
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
summary(fit_manova, test = "Roy")
##             Df     Roy approx F num Df den Df    Pr(>F)    
## state        5 0.57186   312.12      5   2729 < 2.2e-16 ***
## Residuals 2729                                             
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# --- 3) Profile plots ---
means_long = dat %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(mut_vars), names_to = "Rate", values_to = "Value") %>%
  group_by(state, Rate) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean = mean(Value),
    sd = sd(Value),
    se = sd / sqrt(n),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Order the four mutation variables (optional aesthetic)
means_long$Rate = factor(means_long$Rate, levels = mut_vars)




p_all = ggplot(means_long, aes(x = Rate, y = mean, group = state, color = factor(state))) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.8, size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean - 1.96*se, ymax = mean + 1.96*se),
                width = 0.1, alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Profile Plot of Mean Standardized Mutation Rates by Divergence State",
    x = "Mutation Rate",
    y = "Mean (standardized units)",
    color = "State",
    caption = "Error bars are ~95% CIs: mean ± 1.96*SE"
  ) +
  # Distinct color palette
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "1" = "#D55E00",  # red-orange
    "2" = "#E69F00",  # orange
    "3" = "#009E73",  # green
    "4" = "#56B4E9",  # light blue
    "5" = "#0072B2",  # dark blue
    "6" = "#CC79A7"   # purple-pink
  )) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 12)  # center the title
  )

p_all

# --- 2) Focused comparison: State 1 vs State 6 using Hotelling’s T² ---
dat_1_6 = dat %>% filter(state %in% c("1","6"))

fit_manova_1_6 = manova(cbind(ins.std, del.std, sub.std, microsat.std) ~ state, data = dat_1_6)
summary(fit_manova_1_6, test = "Wilks")
##            Df   Wilks approx F num Df den Df    Pr(>F)    
## state       1 0.33083    258.9      4    512 < 2.2e-16 ***
## Residuals 515                                             
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
means_long_1_6 = dat_1_6 %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(mut_vars), names_to = "Rate", values_to = "Value") %>%
  group_by(state, Rate) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean = mean(Value),
    sd = sd(Value),
    se = sd / sqrt(n),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Order the four mutation variables (optional aesthetic)
means_long_1_6$Rate = factor(means_long_1_6$Rate, levels = mut_vars)




p_all_1_6 = ggplot(means_long_1_6, aes(x = Rate, y = mean, group = state, color = factor(state))) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.8, size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean - 1.96*se, ymax = mean + 1.96*se),
                width = 0.1, alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Profile Plot of Mean Standardized Mutation Rates Between 1 and 6",
    x = "Mutation Rate",
    y = "Mean (standardized units)",
    color = "State",
    caption = "Error bars are ~95% CIs: mean ± 1.96*SE"
  ) +
  # Distinct color palette
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "1" = "#D55E00",  # red-orange
    "6" = "#CC79A7"   # purple-pink
  )) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 12)  # center the title
  )

p_all_1_6

# Explicit select to avoid conflict with MASS
mutation_rates <- dplyr::select(genom_data, ins.std, del.std, sub.std, microsat.std)
genomic_features <- dplyr::select(genom_data, GC, CpG, nCGm, LINE, SINE, NLp)

# Run CCA
cca_res <- cc(mutation_rates, genomic_features)

# Test significance of canonical correlations
p.asym(cca_res$cor, 
       nrow(genom_data), 
       ncol(mutation_rates), 
       ncol(genomic_features))
## Wilks' Lambda, using F-approximation (Rao's F):
##               stat   approx df1      df2   p.value
## 1 to 4:  0.9490378 5.984487  24 9507.599 0.0000000
## 2 to 4:  0.9936520 1.158721  15 7525.687 0.2969017
## 3 to 4:  0.9969836 1.030559   8 5454.000 0.4101214
## 4 to 4:  0.9988834 1.016468   3 2728.000 0.3842442
cca_res$cor
## [1] 0.21189421 0.05780711 0.04361181 0.03341510
# Canonical loadings (correlations of original variables with canonical variates)
# Mutation rates with their canonical variates
mut_loadings = cor(mutation_rates, cca_res$scores$xscores)

# Genomic features with their canonical variates
feat_loadings = cor(genomic_features, cca_res$scores$yscores)

# Put into a nice table
mut_loadings
##                   [,1]       [,2]       [,3]        [,4]
## ins.std      0.9461731  0.2933431  0.1367459 -0.00260773
## del.std      0.8771509 -0.3923745  0.1073043  0.25521432
## sub.std      0.8177016 -0.3697848 -0.1589872 -0.41151724
## microsat.std 0.1286335  0.1663830 -0.9104989  0.35603626
feat_loadings
##            [,1]        [,2]       [,3]        [,4]
## GC   -0.3966014  0.57791156  0.1056483 -0.38806092
## CpG  -0.3246485  0.18303190  0.5886000 -0.10865648
## nCGm -0.7987977 -0.29407281 -0.3741971 -0.15311318
## LINE  0.6649031 -0.09879503 -0.3602179  0.06577944
## SINE -0.4471601  0.37457214  0.2552429 -0.24761930
## NLp   0.1137271  0.18358593 -0.3730708  0.84655195
df_scores = data.frame(CCA1_X = cca_res$scores$xscores[,1],
                       CCA2_X = cca_res$scores$xscores[,2],
                       CCA1_Y = cca_res$scores$yscores[,1],
                       CCA2_Y = cca_res$scores$yscores[,2])

# Scatterplot of first pair
ggplot(df_scores, aes(x = CCA1_X, y = CCA1_Y)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5) +
  labs(title = "Canonical Correlation: First Pair")
